paper.title: The origin of High-velocity stars considering the impact of the Large Magellanic Cloud

extraction.summary: Extracted 9 unbound high-velocity star candidates from Table 4, including 7 newly identified by this work and 2 previously reported by Marchetti et al. (2021) and Li et al. (2021). Excluded 57 bound runaway stars, 453 high-velocity halo stars, and Sgr dSph origin candidates that do not meet the P_ub > 0.5 unbound threshold.

method_chain.summary: Adopt astrometry from Gaia DR3 and cross-match with spectroscopic surveys (GALAH DR3, LAMOST DR10, RAVE DR6, APOGEE DR17, S5) for radial velocities.

source_refs.context: Observation sample description of Gaia DR3 and survey cross-matching

method_chain.summary: Apply quality cuts: RUWE < 1.4, parallax zero-point corrected, S/N > 10, remove variable stars, and select single stars using DSC-Combmod probability.

source_refs.context: Quality criteria and flag removal

method_chain.summary: Cross-match Gaia DR3 with large-scale Galactic surveys to obtain spectroscopic radial velocities.

source_refs.context: Cross-matching with spectroscopic surveys

method_chain.summary: Compute weighted mean radial velocity from multiple surveys and filter outliers relative to Gaia DR3 RV.

source_refs.context: Equation 1 and outlier filtering for radial velocity

method_chain.summary: Bayesian distance estimation using a three-dimensional Gaussian likelihood and Bailer-Jones 2021 generalized Gamma prior, sampled with MCMC (emcee).

source_refs.context: Bayesian distance estimation methodology

method_chain.summary: Transform to 3D Galactocentric Cartesian velocities and positions using solar position and velocity, yielding total velocity v_GC and azimuthal velocity v_phi.

source_refs.context: Solar position and velocity adopted for Galactocentric transformation

method_chain.summary: Adopt Gala MilkyWayPotential for the MW (Hernquist bulge/nucleus, Miyamoto-Nagai disk, NFW halo) and Plummer potential for the LMC with dynamical friction.

source_refs.context: Milky Way and LMC potential models

method_chain.summary: Backward orbit integration using galpy over 3 Gyr with 0.1 Myr time step, including 4000 Monte Carlo realizations per object.

source_refs.context: Orbit integration parameters and MC realizations

method_chain.summary: Compute probability P_ub of exceeding the Galactic escape velocity curve from 4000 MC realizations. Classify as unbound if P_ub > 0.5.

source_refs.context: HiVels sample definition including 9 unbound candidates with P_ub greater than 0.5

method_chain.summary: Assess candidate origins by checking proximity to Galactic Center, Sgr dSph half-light radius, Galactic disk, and globular clusters, yielding P_disk and P_Sgr.

source_refs.context: Origin criteria for GC, Sgr dSph, and globular clusters

inclusion_assessment.summary: Paper explicitly identifies Gaia DR3 1309092223502856576 as one of 9 unbound high-velocity star candidates with P_ub = 0.729.
inclusion_assessment.confidence_reason: The object is listed in Table 4, which the paper captions as 'Basic parameters for the 9 unbound candidates', and Section 3 explicitly states these are unbound candidates with P_ub > 0.5.

source_refs.context: Table 4 caption lists basic parameters for the 9 unbound candidates
Section 3 states 519 HiVels include 9 unbound candidates with P_ub greater than 0.5

radial_velocity.description: Weighted mean heliocentric radial velocity from cross-matched spectroscopic surveys

galactocentric_tangential_velocity.description: Azimuthal velocity in the Galactocentric frame

total_velocity.description: Galactocentric total velocity (space velocity relative to the Galactic center)

unbound_probability.description: Probability of exceeding the Galactic escape velocity curve

bound_status_metric.description: Same as P_ub, used as bound/unbound status metric

hypothesis_metrics.description: Probability of the stellar trajectory tracking back to the Galactic disk

candidate_groups_considered.description: 57 bound runaway stars with P_ub < 0.5 and P_disk > 0.5 identified in the 519 HiVels sample.
candidate_groups_considered.reason: The paper classifies these as bound stars ejected from the Galactic disk, not as Galactic-unbound candidates.

source_refs.context: Section 4.1 states 57 bound HiVels are runaway stars

candidate_groups_considered.description: 453 high-velocity halo stars with P_ub < 0.5 from the 519 HiVels sample.
candidate_groups_considered.reason: The paper classifies these as bound high-velocity halo stars that may form within the MW or have extragalactic origins, but they do not meet the P_ub > 0.5 unbound threshold.

source_refs.context: Section 4.1 states remaining 453 HiVels are high-velocity halo stars

candidate_groups_considered.description: High-velocity stars in Table 5 with potential origin from the Sagittarius dwarf spheroidal galaxy.
candidate_groups_considered.reason: These stars are discussed as Sgr dSph origin candidates but are not among the 9 unbound candidates with P_ub > 0.5 presented in Table 4. The paper does not present them as Galactic-unbound candidates in this work.

source_refs.context: Section 4.3 discusses Sgr dSph origin stars in Table 5
